faqs.question: What types of spiders are common in South Jamaica homes?
faqs.answer: South Jamaica residents most frequently encounter house spiders, cellar spiders, and occasionally wolf spiders. During warmer months, you may also see jumping spiders around windows and doors. Our treatments target all common species found in Queens residential properties.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide spider control service in South Jamaica?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to South Jamaica properties in most cases. Our team serves the entire neighborhood, from areas near York College to homes closer to JFK Airport, with flexible scheduling including evenings and weekends.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 spider control service include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive spider control includes a thorough inspection, interior and exterior treatment of entry points and hiding spots, web removal, and preventative barriers. The price varies based on property size and infestation severity. We provide a detailed quote after our initial assessment.

faqs.question: Are your spider control treatments safe for families and pets in South Jamaica homes?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-registered products that are safe for children and pets when applied by our licensed professionals. Treatments are targeted to areas where spiders hide while minimizing exposure to living spaces. We provide specific safety instructions for your particular treatment plan.
